title: D1 regime-conditional sparse (CUSUM) primaries are event-starved below the WF floor — looser variants insufficient; needs H4 or per-episode criterion (T011D2 + T015D2)
why: Two independent Loop A survivors now falsified on the SAME walk-forward refusal cliff, not on edge: T011D2 (COT+CUSUM AND, long) fired 35 events in BULL_QUIET; T015D2 (CUSUM-down + volume>=0.60, short) fired 73 in BEAR_QUIET. The WF floor at the relaxed Loop A geometry (n_folds=2) is train_min(~36) + 2x100 = ~236 events; both fall far short. CONCRETE PROJECTION 2026-05-27 (B0048 lesson applied — counted events before auditing): of 308 raw T015D2 short events over 22y, the per-regime split is BULL_QUIET 157 / BEAR_QUIET 73 / BULL_STRESSED 28 / BEAR_STRESSED 2. BEAR_STRESSED has only 47 bars in the entire 22y history, so the proposed wider-scope re-run (BEAR_QUIET+BEAR_STRESSED) yields just 73->75 events — still FAILS 236. No coherent regime-conditional scope reaches 236: even the largest single regime (BULL_QUIET, 157) falls short, and removing the volume gate within BEAR_QUIET only ~doubles to ~146 (CUSUM is a sparse event detector by design, AFML 3.3). Only pooling all 4 regimes (~308) clears the floor, but that abandons regime-conditioning and defeats the Phase 5 premise. CONCLUSION: looser within-regime variants (the original B0049 idea) are INSUFFICIENT on D1. The real fixes are structural: (a) move these hypotheses to H4 (Paso 2), where ~4-6x bar density per regime lifts event counts past the floor; or (b) make the per-episode survival sign-test the PRIMARY robustness criterion (the proposals already declare per_episode_survival_fraction / per_episode_min_trades, but make_folds raises the WF geometry error BEFORE any per-episode logic runs — an architectural mismatch: the pipeline enforces WF-Sharpe folds, not the per-episode criterion the hypothesis committed to). A bare all-4-pooled run is only useful as a non-regime-conditional baseline (does this primary have ANY edge), a different question. H4 RE-HOME REFUTED 2026-05-27 (projected before building, B0048 discipline): H4 XAUUSD is only 5.0y (2021-05 -> 2026-05, 7740 bars) and that window is predominantly the gold BULL market, so BEAR_QUIET is RARE on H4: just 399 bars (5.2%) vs D1 1286 (22.8%). The primary fires only 31 events in H4 BEAR_QUIET, all inside ONE 3-month episode (2023-09 -> 2024-01) — far below the H4 WF floor (n_folds=2, train_min=50 -> needs ~250). So H4 makes it WORSE (31 < D1 73), not better: the 4-6x per-time density is more than offset by 5y vs 22y history, and the 2013-15 gold bear (where most D1 BEAR_QUIET events live) is simply absent from the H4 window. No extended XAU H4 dataset exists (only XAG/ETH/BTC are extended). Even the densest H4 regime, BULL_QUIET at 224 events, narrowly misses the 250 floor. NET: neither D1 nor H4 XAU can audit a regime-conditional sparse primary via walk-forward. The per-episode sign-test path is now the ONLY viable single-asset D1 route; cross-asset to longer/deeper-bear-history instruments (e.g. XAGUSD D1, gated on B0009 cross-asset regime parquets) is the alternative generalization route.
value_rationale: M: the causal story is plausible and orthogonal to trend, but it is one hypothesis among many and the looser variants risk diluting the original mechanism, so payoff is uncertain.
scope: Decide the structural fix: (a) re-home sparse regime-conditional primaries to H4, or (b) wire the per-episode sign-test as a first-class audit path that runs when WF geometry is infeasible.
If (b): make_folds / _run_one_primary must detect WF-infeasibility and fall through to a per-episode evaluator instead of raising, gated on the proposal declaring per_episode_* criteria.
Looser within-regime variants (lower volume_pct_threshold, OR-gate, lower cusum mult) are documented INSUFFICIENT on D1 — do not pursue as the primary fix; only as H4 tuning.
Optional: a non-regime-conditional all-4-pooled baseline run to measure whether the bare primary has any unconditioned edge (separate question from the regime hypothesis).
out_of_scope: Re-auditing T011D2 with relaxed walk-forward geometry — that would override its committed falsification floor.

external_refs.label: T015D2 (falsified on same event floor: 73<236; BEAR_STRESSED only 47 bars)

history.from: Less-selective COT+CUSUM primary variant — D1 AND-gate too sparse to audit
history.to: D1 regime-conditional sparse (CUSUM) primaries are event-starved below the WF floor — looser variants insufficient; needs H4 or per-episode criterion (T011D2 + T015D2)

history.reason: T015D2 corroboration + projection data + structural conclusion
history.from: T011D2's primary ANDs two restrictive conditions — COT net-noncomm 52w-z rolling-quantile >= 0.75 AND a CUSUM(2*ATR14) upward event, long-only — which yields only 35 events over 22y in BULL_QUIET on XAU D1: too sparse for the walk-forward machinery. The mechanism (speculative conviction tier conditioning continuation) may still hold; the gating is just too tight for D1. Candidate looser variants to make it auditable without goalpost-moving on T011D2 itself: lower cot_quantile_floor to 0.50, OR-combine the two conditions instead of AND, lower cusum_h_atr_mult, or move to H4 where event density is higher. Each variant needs its own ex-ante falsification criterion committed before any run.
history.to: Two independent Loop A survivors now falsified on the SAME walk-forward refusal cliff, not on edge: T011D2 (COT+CUSUM AND, long) fired 35 events in BULL_QUIET; T015D2 (CUSUM-down + volume>=0.60, short) fired 73 in BEAR_QUIET. The WF floor at the relaxed Loop A geometry (n_folds=2) is train_min(~36) + 2x100 = ~236 events; both fall far short. CONCRETE PROJECTION 2026-05-27 (B0048 lesson applied — counted events before auditing): of 308 raw T015D2 short events over 22y, the per-regime split is BULL_QUIET 157 / BEAR_QUIET 73 / BULL_STRESSED 28 / BEAR_STRESSED 2. BEAR_STRESSED has only 47 bars in the entire 22y history, so the proposed wider-scope re-run (BEAR_QUIET+BEAR_STRESSED) yields just 73->75 events — still FAILS 236. No coherent regime-conditional scope reaches 236: even the largest single regime (BULL_QUIET, 157) falls short, and removing the volume gate within BEAR_QUIET only ~doubles to ~146 (CUSUM is a sparse event detector by design, AFML 3.3). Only pooling all 4 regimes (~308) clears the floor, but that abandons regime-conditioning and defeats the Phase 5 premise. CONCLUSION: looser within-regime variants (the original B0049 idea) are INSUFFICIENT on D1. The real fixes are structural: (a) move these hypotheses to H4 (Paso 2), where ~4-6x bar density per regime lifts event counts past the floor; or (b) make the per-episode survival sign-test the PRIMARY robustness criterion (the proposals already declare per_episode_survival_fraction / per_episode_min_trades, but make_folds raises the WF geometry error BEFORE any per-episode logic runs — an architectural mismatch: the pipeline enforces WF-Sharpe folds, not the per-episode criterion the hypothesis committed to). A bare all-4-pooled run is only useful as a non-regime-conditional baseline (does this primary have ANY edge), a different question.

history.reason: Load-bearing: blocks ALL Loop A D1 sparse-primary survivors from reaching a Sharpe verdict, not one hypothesis
Reframed around structural fixes after within-regime relaxation shown insufficient
from: Draft 1-2 looser variants (lower quantile floor, OR-gate, or H4 timeframe) as new proposals.
Each must commit its own falsification_criterion (including a realistic n_trades_total_min) ex ante.
Re-run pre-flight + M3 audit per variant.
to: Decide the structural fix: (a) re-home sparse regime-conditional primaries to H4, or (b) wire the per-episode sign-test as a first-class audit path that runs when WF geometry is infeasible.
If (b): make_folds / _run_one_primary must detect WF-infeasibility and fall through to a per-episode evaluator instead of raising, gated on the proposal declaring per_episode_* criteria.
Looser within-regime variants (lower volume_pct_threshold, OR-gate, lower cusum mult) are documented INSUFFICIENT on D1 — do not pursue as the primary fix; only as H4 tuning.
Optional: a non-regime-conditional all-4-pooled baseline run to measure whether the bare primary has any unconditioned edge (separate question from the regime hypothesis).
